description: Implement canonical module docs rendering for the grouped-query attention page: localized message keys, resolved asset references, registry-backed tag pills, citations, and a minimal same-variant-group derived related-documents section inside the standard docs shell.

context.customerAsk: Phase 1: implement canonical docs rendering for the grouped-query attention module page using localized message keys, resolved asset references, tag pills, citations, and a minimal derived related-documents section.
context.problem: Model Atlas defines canonical docs as MDX structure plus colocated messages, assets, and registry records, but readers cannot yet open the grouped-query attention module page and see localized text, resolved media, tag pills, citations, and taxonomy-derived related links composed in the docs shell. Phase 1 cannot validate the content model without one end-to-end reference page.
context.solution: Wire Fumadocs MDX to docs feature components, resolve colocated messages and assets at render time using src/lib/content loaders from content-registry-validation, and publish the grouped-query attention page following docs/templates/module.mdx. Render TagPillList, CitationList, and minimal DerivedRelatedDocs (same-variant-group) from registry data so this page becomes the reusable pattern for future module docs.

acceptanceCriteria: A reader can open /docs/modules/grouped-query-attention locally and see a complete module reference inside the standard docs shell.
Visible title, problem statement, core idea, and section copy render from colocated messages/en.json via message-key components, not raw MDX prose.
At least one assetId on the GQA page resolves to visible media with alt text from message keys.
Tag pills and citations on the GQA page render from registry data with working navigation and outbound citation links.
DerivedRelatedDocs shows same-variant-group with per-link reason labels when peers exist; empty groups are omitted.
Automated tests cover message resolution, asset resolution, citation MLA rendering, and variant-group related-doc derivation.
Typecheck passes, lint passes, and tests pass for all changes in this work item.

userStories.title: Message-key rendering in docs MDX
userStories.description: As a reader, I want page text to resolve from localized message files so MDX stays structural and future locales can ship without rewriting templates.
acceptanceCriteria: Fumadocs MDX component map exposes T and Section with access to the active page colocated messages (default locale en).
T renders strings by key; Section renders headings from titleKey.
Missing required keys surface a clear developer-visible error in development, not silent empty production output.
Automated tests cover message lookup happy path and missing-key behavior.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Asset resolution and rendering by asset ID
userStories.description: As a reader, I want diagrams and images to load from declared asset config so maintainers never scatter media paths through MDX.
acceptanceCriteria: A valid assetId on the GQA page renders a visible asset slot using colocated assets.json.
Alt text and captions resolve through message keys referenced by asset config.
Invalid assetId or broken asset config fails build-time validation or shows an explicit development error state.
Automated tests cover asset resolution for at least one image-type and one graph-type fixture.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Publish and render the grouped-query attention module page
userStories.description: As a reader researching attention variants, I want a grouped-query attention page that follows the canonical module template so I can learn what GQA is and why it matters.
acceptanceCriteria: src/content/docs/modules/grouped-query-attention/page.mdx follows the module template with registryId module.grouped-query-attention and local message and asset namespaces.
Problem statement and core idea render below the title via message keys only.
ModuleMetadataCard and ModuleAtAGlance display registry-backed fields for the GQA record.
Navigating to /docs/modules/grouped-query-attention returns HTTP 200 with the docs shell in a local dev build.
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: title and opening paragraphs match messages/en.json, not hard-coded MDX prose.

userStories.title: Clickable tag pills on the module page
userStories.description: As a reader, I want to click tags on the module page to explore related topics such as attention and kv-cache.
acceptanceCriteria: TagPillList renders one keyboard-focusable pill per tag on the module registry record.
Each pill links to /tags/<slug> or an equivalent search URL with that tag applied.
Tag pills use restrained monochrome styling per site fundamentals.
When the registry record has no tags, TagPillList renders nothing.
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: GQA page shows expected tags and each pill navigates to a working destination.

userStories.title: Citations in the References section
userStories.description: As a reader, I want cited sources with MLA formatting so technical claims on the module page are grounded.
acceptanceCriteria: CitationList renders each citationId on the module record with MLA text from citation registry records.
Each citation includes an outbound link with rel=noopener noreferrer to the canonical source URL.
When citationIds is empty, References shows a concise empty state or omits the list without broken markup.
Automated tests cover MLA rendering for at least one fixture citation record.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser: GQA References lists expected sources with working links.

userStories.title: Minimal derived related documents
userStories.description: As a reader comparing attention mechanisms, I want nearby variants grouped with clear reasons so authors do not hand-maintain related link lists in MDX.
acceptanceCriteria: DerivedRelatedDocs on the GQA page renders same-variant-group when peer module records exist.
Each related item shows a title link and a visible reason label such as Same variant group.
Groups with zero matches are not rendered.
Automated tests verify variant-group peers are selected for a fixture registry set.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser: GQA page shows related attention variants with reason labels when peer records exist.
